channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Trump pode ajudar Lula de novo se insistir em pressionar por mudança no PIX
item.description: Lula reaffirmed his support for Brazil’s PIX instant payment system during a speech in Bahia, rejecting external pressures to change it following a new U.S. government report criticizing the tool for harming American credit card companies. The defense of PIX, previously used by Lula to gain political traction amid U.S. trade pressures, may again boost his popularity amid rising disapproval, especially as it is framed as a nationalist issue against Bolsonaro-linked opposition. Petista activists are now linking the criticism of PIX to PL presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro.

item.title: "Pix é do Brasil e ninguém vai fazer a gente mudar", diz Lula ao citar relatório dos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated on Thursday (2) that Brazil's Pix instant payment system, launched in 2020 as a free service, will not be changed despite a U.S. report on the matter. He emphasized that 'Pix is Brazil's and nobody will make us change it'.

item.title: Sob críticas de Lula, Petrobras estuda cancelar leilão de gás de cozinha que teve ágio de 117%
item.description: Petrobras is studying the cancellation of a cooking gas auction after criticism from President Lula, as the bidding ended with premiums above 100%, raising concerns about the impact on final consumer prices.

item.title: Lula rebate Trump, diz que Pix é do Brasil e não será alterado
item.description: President Lula stated that Pix is a Brazilian creation and will not be changed due to external pressure, following U.S. criticism in the USTR report. He emphasized that the government can improve Pix to better serve the population but will not yield to international demands labeling it as trade-distorting. The remarks were made during a visit to Salvador’s VLT construction site, after a direct request from Secom Minister Sidônio Palmeira.

item.title: Como as mudanças no chocolate podem impactar os futuros ovos de Páscoa
item.description: A bill approved by the Chamber of Deputies seeks to regulate the minimum cocoa content in chocolates in Brazil, aiming to counteract ultraprocessing practices that reduce cocoa and increase sugar and fat, making products like Easter eggs overly sweet and less authentic. The proposal sets specific cocoa percentages for each chocolate type (dark, milk, white, etc.) and requires these figures to be visible on over 15% of the front packaging. The text now moves to the Senate for debate, where, if passed, it will be sent to President Lula for sanction or veto.

item.title: Conflitos por preços de diesel e gás pressionam governo
item.description: President Lula announced he will annul Petrobras' LPG auction, calling it 'cretinice' and 'bandidagem' due to over 100% agio, stating the poor will not pay for the war's costs. Meanwhile, fuel distributors Vibra, Ipiranga, and Raízen refuse to join the diesel subsidy program, maintaining high profits despite pressure to lower fuel taxes. Eduardo Bolsonaro threatens to report alleged TSE bias to the Trump administration, while Minister Toffoli faces criticism for using private jets for official travel.

item.title: Senadora alega insatisfação com candidatura presidencial de Caiado, deixa PSD e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (MA) left the PSD and joined the PT following the confirmation of Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential pre-candidacy by the same party, stating the PSD had taken a 'new track' with a more right-wing figure. She aims to help Lula win re-election in October and had been part of the government base in the Senate, serving as a bridge to the evangelical community. Her departure reduces the PSD’s Senate bench from 13 to 11 senators, following Rodrigo Pacheco’s move to the PSB.

item.title: Lula quer mandar bancos darem desconto em dívidas, diz Durigan
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan announced the government is studying guarantees to enable banks and fintechs to apply broad discounts on debt renegotiations, reducing the outstanding balance before refinancing, with potential financial education requirements. The initiative, a priority of President Lula, aims to simplify access relative to the Desenrola Brasil program, functioning as a risk-reducing guarantee rather than a subsidy.

item.title: Gonet defende livrar Moro de inclusão no Inquérito das Milícias Digitais
item.description: Attorney General Paulo Gonet recommended that the STF reject the request to include Senator Sergio Moro in the Digital Militias Inquiry, stating that the petitioner, federal deputy Lindbergh Farias, lacks active legitimacy to bring the case before the court. The final decision rests with Minister Alexandre de Moraes, who has not yet ruled. Moro, a pre-candidate for governor of Paraná, had made unsubstantiated claims that the 2022 election was illegitimate, prompting the inquiry request.

item.title: Lula reforça soberania sobre o Pix nas redes após críticas dos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva reaffirmed Brazil's sovereignty over the Pix payment system following U.S. criticism that it distorts international trade. He posted on social media: 'Pix is Brazil's' and declared at an event in Salvador that 'no one will make Brazil change the Pix'.

item.title: A conversa de Lula e Pacheco antes de filiação do senador ao PSB
item.description: One hour before joining the PSB, Senator Rodrigo Pacheco spoke with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who wished him luck in his new party. The expectation within the party and from Lula is that Pacheco will run for governor of Minas Gerais and provide a political platform for Lula’s re-election bid in the state. Although he did not confirm his candidacy, Pacheco indicated that talks to form a progressive slate in Minas will begin next week.

item.title: Combo que liga STF a Vorcaro pressiona PGR a investigar ministros
item.description: Alleged connections between Supreme Court ministers and former banker Daniel Vorcaro are increasing pressure on the PGR to investigate potential irregularities tied to Banco Master. The revealed links involve suspicions of favoritism and conflicts of interest, sparking debates over judicial independence. The Prosecutor General's Office faces demands to deepen its inquiries within ongoing investigations.

item.title: Alckmin diz que 'quem defende ditadura não devia nem ser candidato'
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin stated that those who defend dictatorship should not even be candidates, referring to PL's pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro. He announced his departure from the MDIC to run on Lula's ticket in 2026, arguing that elections will be decided once campaigning begins and criticizing Brazil's excessive multiparty system.

item.title: "Pix é do Brasil e ninguém vai fazer a gente mudar", diz Lula após críticas da Casa Branca
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended Brazil's Pix instant payment system as a national asset, rejecting White House claims that it creates unfair competition for U.S. firms like Visa and Mastercard. Lula stated that 'nobody will make us change Pix', highlighting its role in financial inclusion and serving the needs of the Brazilian population. The remarks were made during a Novo PAC event in Salvador, following a prompt from Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira, and were later amplified on social media with the slogan 'Pix é do Brasil'.

item.title: Lula rebate críticas do governo Trump: ‘Ninguém nos fará mudar o Pix’
item.description: President Lula defended Brazil's Pix instant payment system during a visit to urban mobility works in Salvador, stating that no one will make Brazil change Pix despite criticisms from the Trump administration. He also labeled opposition groups as 'fascists' and vowed to prevent their return to power, linking adversaries like Flávio Bolsonaro to Trump's decisions as part of the PT's electoral strategy.

item.title: Como Lula trabalha para adiantar a sabatina de Messias no Senado
item.description: President Lula is working to speed up the confirmation hearing of Jorge Messias in the Senate, aiming for his approval to the Supreme Federal Court before the 2026 elections. Although he has not met personally with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, Lula seeks to ensure the nominee already has the minimum support needed for approval in the Constitution and Justice Committee and later in the plenary. Messias will be on vacation for the next three weeks but will remain in Brasília to gather votes.

item.title: Novo vídeo expõe armas da campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro contra Lula
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro released a 53-second pre-campaign video focused on the economy, criticizing rising fuel and electricity costs, and proposing a leaner state with lower taxes to benefit workers and businesses. Although he did not mention Lula directly, the video indirectly criticized the current government by rejecting 'populist promises' and advocating for 'results'. The campaign avoided traditional bolsonarist themes such as civic-military schools or attacks on electronic voting machines, concentrating solely on the country's economic situation.

item.title: Relator da PEC da Segurança se filia ao PL em meio à expectativa de Lula de aprovar proposta
item.description: Federal Deputy Mendonça Filho (PE) left the União Brasil party and joined the Liberal Party (PL) on April 1st, announcing the move in a video alongside Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for President. He stated he will continue as 'a voice of opposition to the PT'. The affiliation comes amid President Lula’s expectation that the Public Security PEC, of which Mendonça is the rapporteur, be approved in the Senate as a precondition to recreating the Ministry of Public Security by June — a move seen as strategic to bolster his 2026 re-election campaign.

item.title: Governo Lula é aprovado por 55,7% no CE, diz Paraná Pesquisas
item.description: According to a Paraná Pesquisas survey released on April 2, 2026, President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s government is approved by 55.7% of voters in Ceará, with 40.7% disapproving and 3.6% undecided. In detailed evaluation, 40.8% rate the administration as 'excellent' or 'good', 23.9% as 'fair', and 33.7% as 'bad' or 'terrible'. The poll interviewed 1,500 people across 68 municipalities from March 27–30, 2026, with a 2.6 percentage point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Lula lidera disputa pelo Planalto no Ceará, diz pesquisa
item.description: Lula leads the presidential race in Ceará in both the first and second rounds, according to a Paraná Pesquisas survey released in April 2026. The poll, conducted with 1,500 voters across 68 municipalities from March 27–30, has a margin of error of 2.6 percentage points and a 95% confidence level. Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL’s pre-candidate, is shown as his main opponent.

item.title: Caiado escolhe SP como base da campanha e aposta em comparação de currículos com Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado has chosen São Paulo as the base for his presidential pre-campaign under the PSD, prioritizing media concentration and operational convenience, with meetings scheduled with Gilberto Kassab and strategist Paulo Vasconcelos to finalize strategy, including TV ads in May. Despite Goiás being his electoral stronghold, Caiado is positioning himself against Flávio Bolsonaro by emphasizing curriculum comparison to capture the right-wing electorate, while engaging with his former party, União Brasil, and aiming for a pure PSD ticket.

item.title: Lula defende o Pix de críticas dos EUA após lembrança de Sidônio: ‘É do Brasil e não vamos mudar’
item.description: President Lula defended Brazil's Pix payment system against U.S. criticisms that it distorts international trade, stating the system is Brazilian and will not be changed. He acknowledged the system could be improved to better serve Brazilians but ruled out structural alterations. The remarks came after being reminded by Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira during a VLT event in Salvador, Bahia.

item.title: 'Ninguém vai fazer a gente mudar o PIX', diz Lula ao comentar relatório dos EUA com críticas à ferramenta
item.description: President Lula stated that no one will make Brazil change the PIX, despite U.S. criticism in a report calling it harmful to credit card giants. He emphasized that PIX is a Brazilian tool benefiting society and can be improved to better serve social needs. The remark was made during a visit to VLT works in Salvador, following a reminder from Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira.

item.title: Governo Lula quer acelerar fim da escala 6 X 1 com PL próprio
item.description: The Lula government will send its own bill to Congress to end the 6x1 work schedule and establish a 40-hour weekly workweek without salary reduction, using constitutional urgency before Labor Day. The bill aims to speed up approval, avoiding the risk of a constitutional amendment (PEC) stalling in Congress, and unifies existing proposals such as PEC 221 by Hugo Motta and the one by Erika Hilton. This move is part of Lula’s 2026 electoral strategy, framing reduced work hours as a key campaign banner.

item.title: Lula chama de ‘bandidagem’ aumento da gasolina
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva labeled the recent increase in gasoline and alcohol prices as 'gangsterism' by certain individuals, stating there is no justification for the hikes. He also announced he will annul a Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) auction conducted by Petrobras, claiming it was carried out against government guidance and unfairly burdens the poor.

item.title: ‘Bandidagem’: Lula diz que anulará leilão de gás de cozinha
item.description: President Lula announced that his government will annul the Petrobras auction that sold cooking gas at prices over 100% above reference levels, calling the operation 'cretinism and banditry'. He stated the auction contradicted government guidance to not raise LPG prices and emphasized that poor citizens will not bear the cost linked to the Iran war. Lula also connected the event to earlier privatizations of Petrobras refineries and highlighted federal efforts to mitigate Middle East crisis impacts, including diesel tax exemptions and talks with governors to lower ICMS on the product.

item.title: As intenções de voto de Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro no Ceará, segundo nova pesquisa
item.description: A new Paraná Pesquisas poll in Ceará shows President Lula (PT) leading with 52.7% of voting intentions in the first round and 55.3% in the second round against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), who has 29.7% and 33.7% respectively. The survey interviewed 1,500 voters between March 27 and 30, with a margin of error of 2.6 percentage points and a 95% confidence level. Other candidates had negligible support, while 7.7% would vote blank/null or were undecided in the first round.

item.title: Deputada aciona PGR após Flávio dizer que terras raras do Brasil são ‘solução’ aos EUA
item.description: A deputy filed a complaint with the Attorney General's Office (PGR) after Flávio Bolsonaro claimed Brazil could be the solution to reduce U.S. dependence on Chinese rare earths and critical minerals. The statement drew criticism for being perceived as entreguista, particularly from left-wing sectors. The full article is available only to subscribers.

item.title: Senadora deixa PSD após candidatura de Caiado e segue para o PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (MA) left the PSD after the party decided to launch Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential candidacy for 2026. She announced her affiliation with the PT, the party of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, in a formal act on Thursday (02/02/2026). The move reflects a political realignment amid internal disputes in the PSD and the party’s selection of Caiado as its presidential nominee.

item.title: Lula quer anular leilão da Petrobras por vender gás acima da tabela
item.description: President Lula said he will annul the Petrobras auction that sold cooking gas at up to 100% above the state table price, calling it 'cretinism' and 'banditry'. He stated the auction was conducted against the will of Petrobras leadership and that poor people will not bear such prices. The government already has the Gás do Povo program to provide free gas cylinders to low-income families.

item.title: Eliziane agora vai defender Lula de dentro do PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama announced her departure from the PSD and plans to join the PT to defend President Lula’s government from within the party. She expressed gratitude for her time in the PSD but reaffirmed her commitment to social justice in Maranhão. The move comes amid political realignments ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Moraes aperta o cerco contra drones sobre a casa de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes expanded the drone flight ban to a one-kilometer radius around former President Jair Bolsonaro's residence in Brasília, following a request from the DF Military Police. The ruling authorizes the PM to shoot down, seize drones, and arrest operators in flagrante, with immediate reporting to the STF. The measure aims to prevent unlawful surveillance and threats to institutional security.

item.title: Sem citar Flávio, Lula diz que é o ano da verdade e que cada um tem que mostrar o que fez
item.description: President Lula declared 2026 as the 'year of truth,' urging candidates to show voters what they have already done for the people, without naming Flávio Bolsonaro but making clear the message was aimed at him. He said the era of lies and online misinformation is over, and anyone seeking office must be serious or will be exposed, emphasizing that truth lies in past actions — such as ending hunger in 2014.

item.title: Crítico de federação, Mendonça Filho deixa União Brasil e se filia ao PL em apoio a Flávio
item.description: Federal deputy Mendonça Filho left União Brasil to join the Liberal Party (PL) in support of Flávio Bolsonaro, after opposing a proposed federation between União Brasil and Progressistas. He stated his move reflects his commitment to liberty, responsibility, and opposition to the PT, and that he will run for re-election in 2026 from Pernambuco, advocating economic growth, social development, and public security. His affiliation was praised by Valdemar Costa Neto, who highlighted Mendonça’s past as Education Minister and author of a Public Security PEC.

item.title: Leilão que aumenta preço do GLP vai ser anulado, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula announced that the Petrobras LPG auction, which could have raised the price of a 13kg cylinder by up to R$39.40, will be annulled, calling it a 'cretinage' and 'insubordination' carried out against the state company's direction. He emphasized that the poor will not bear the cost and confirmed leadership changes in Petrobras' Commercial Management Directorate.

item.title: Lula enaltece diálogo com prefeitos e ataca as notícias falsas
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva emphasized dialogue with municipal mayors in an interview with TV Record Bahia, stating that no government in Brazil's history has listened to local officials as much as his current administration. He also attacked the spread of fake news, defending the importance of truth in public discourse.

item.title: Não acaba em pizza
item.description: The Rome Court of Appeal rejected the defense arguments of former deputy Carla Zambelli, which were based on disinformation, and approved her extradition to Brazil. Zambelli was sentenced by the STF to ten years in prison for invading the CNJ’s electronic system in partnership with hacker Walter Delgatti during the 2022 campaign to cast doubt on election integrity. After nine months in Italian custody awaiting the ruling, her lawyers plan to appeal to the Court of Cassation, but European jurists and Brazilian authorities view the decision as a legal landmark.

item.title: As coisas como elas são
item.description: Gisele Agnelli's 'Autocracia Made in USA' examines the second Trump administration as a real-time autocratic project, documenting the erosion of U.S. democratic institutions through 2025 columns and interviews. As a Latin American immigrant political scientist, she offers a sharp comparative lens, linking U.S. democratic backsliding to Brazil's own political crises.

item.title: Venha quem vier
item.description: Edinho Silva, president of the Workers' Party (PT), states that Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in polls was expected due to polarization and the weight of the Bolsonaro surname. He denies the PT has a preference for specific opponents, downplays Ronaldo Caiado's impact, and argues that Lula's 2026 campaign will present a modern, forward-looking program aimed at younger generations, expecting a shift in voter sentiment by mid-year.

item.title: Moraes aumenta área de restrição de drones no entorno da residência de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes expanded the drone no-fly zone to a 1-kilometer radius around Jair Bolsonaro's residence in Brasília, authorizing the Federal District Military Police to shoot down and seize violating drones, with operators facing arrest in flagrante. The measure aims to prevent unauthorized surveillance and protect privacy, while Bolsonaro, sentenced to 27 years and three months for attempting a coup, awaits a ruling on house arrest due to hospitalization, under conditions including electronic monitoring and bans on cell phones and social media.

item.title: STF/ Oásis ou miragem?
item.description: Minister Flávio Dino of the STF ordered a preliminary investigation into possible irregularities in the allocation of R$ 3.6 million in amendments by Senator Carlos Viana to the Fundação Oásis, linked to the Lagoinha Baptist Church, citing 'lack of transparency and traceability'. The case may be referred to the Federal Police if evidence of purpose diversion or favoritism is found. In a separate matter, state deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP) is under criminal investigation by the MPF for blackface and transphobia after a speech in the São Paulo State Assembly questioning the legitimacy of trans deputy Érika Hilton as head of the Women's Rights Defense Committee.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula, Rui Costa e Jerônimo visitam obras do VLT de Salvador
item.description: President Lula will visit the VLT construction site in Salvador on Thursday, April 2, 2026, alongside Governor Jerônimo Rodrigues and Minister Rui Costa. The event will include the signing of the service order for Line 1 of the metro, a R$1.52 billion investment under the Novo PAC.

item.title: A palavra de ordem na campanha de Caiado em relação a Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: In Ronaldo Caiado's campaign, the political strategy draws a distinction between adversaries and competitors: Lula is framed as an ideological adversary, while Flávio Bolsonaro is seen as a direct competitor for the same conservative and right-wing electorate. This differentiation suggests Caiado aims to position himself as an alternative to Bolsonarism without directly attacking its leader, focusing on capturing Bolsonaro's voter base while maintaining clear opposition to the PT. The message reflects an attempt to navigate the polarized landscape with electoral tact.

item.title: TSE julgará recurso de deputado da motosserra na próxima 5ª feira
item.description: The TSE will rule on Thursday, April 9, 2026, on an appeal by Hildebrando Pascoal Nogueira Neto, known as the 'chainsaw deputy', seeking to overturn his 14-year electoral conviction for corruption and criminal association, finalized in 2004. He has been imprisoned since 1999 for leading a death squad in Acre responsible for murders, torture, and dismemberment with a chainsaw, including the 1996 killing of mechanic Agilson Firmino and his 13-year-old son. The appeal, filed as a regimental challenge, contests the monocratic decision of Minister André Ramos Tavares that denied the criminal revision, with rapporteur Minister Estela Aranha.

item.title: Lula reforça pedido a Trump para extradição de empresário dono da Refit
item.description: President Lula reiterated his request to the United States for the extradition of Ricardo Magro, owner of the Manguinhos Refinery (Refit), who is accused of being a leader of organized crime and owing over R$26 billion in ICMS taxes. Magro, currently residing in Miami, is under investigation for allegedly linking the Refit group to tax fraud schemes and supplying fuel to gas stations controlled by the PCC. In the same interview, Lula emphasized China’s willingness to strengthen economic ties with Brazil.

item.title: Após PSD lançar pré-candidatura de Caiado, senadora deixa o partido e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama left the PSD and joined the PT following the launch of Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential pre-candidacy, which repositioned the party as an alternative to Lula’s government. Her departure is part of a broader PSD realignment, after Rodrigo Pacheco also left for the PSB. As a key ally of the government and advocate for Jorge Messias’ STF nomination, Eliziane strengthens Lula’s coalition in Maranhão and supports the pre-candidacy of Vice Governor Felipe Camarão.

item.title: Moraes amplia para 1 km restrição a drones sobre casa de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes expanded the drone restriction radius around former President Jair Bolsonaro’s residence from 100 meters to 1 kilometer, approving a request from the Federal District Military Police. The decision, issued on Thursday (April 2), was based on a technical assessment concluding the previous limit was inadequate to ensure the protective measure’s effectiveness, given drones’ ability to capture images from greater distances.

item.title: Terceira via à moda brasileira
item.description: The text critiques the notion that candidates like Caiado, Zema, or Aldo Rebelo represent a 'Third Way' in Brazil's 2026 elections, arguing they are aligned with reactionary, anarcho-liberal, or nationalist right-wing ideologies. It asserts that the true Third Way—historically a moderate path between extreme neoliberalism and excessive welfare statism—is today embodied by the PT and PSB, which form the governing coalition, and notes that the PSDB once held this profile. It concludes that labeling third-party candidates as a 'Third Way' is an illusion, as they do not offer a genuine alternative to the polarized Lula vs. bolsonarismo dynamic.

item.title: Após lançamento de candidatura de Caiado à Presidência, senadora Eliziane Gama troca PSD pelo PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (MA) left the PSD and joined the PT after Ronaldo Caiado was officially nominated as the party's presidential candidate. She announced the decision on Instagram, stating that while she respects the PSD's new direction, her thinking differs from the party's. Caiado's candidacy has been largely avoided by PSD governors, with only Ratinho Júnior initially offering public support, while Eduardo Leite initially criticized the move before moderating his stance.

item.title: Paralisa ou endividamento? O que explica queda na popularidade do governo
item.description: The decline in President Lula’s popularity is linked to rising household debt, with half of Brazilian families indebted and 82 million in default, according to the Central Bank and Serasa. Economist Alex Agostini argues that the lack of structural fiscal adjustment is forcing consumption to rely on credit rather than income, worsened by economic slowdown and low job creation, a trend expected to persist through 2026.
